_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, which averages the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice indexed to 2015 as 100, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase since 2015. This rise reflects a recent and uneven climb in prices across these commodities. The index summarises global commodity price movements specifically in these soft commodities. Despite the aggregation, individual changes vary significantly from item to item.
notes.movers: Coffee has nearly doubled since 2015 with a 99% increase, making it the largest contributor to the basket's rise. Orange juice prices are up 34%, wheat has increased 19%, and sugar 13%. These shifts are primarily driven by specific factors like weather events and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice, and frost or drought conditions in arabica coffee regions. Since soft commodities trade in comparatively thin markets, these supply shocks tend to cause sharp price fluctuations rather than broad inflation trends.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is largely attributable to a few weather-vulnerable crops whose supply disruptions markedly affect global prices. Given the nature of these agricultural factors, elevated input costs can persist over multiple years, sustaining the upward price pressure within these soft commodities.
